parsed.clarification_question: Does 'review this section' mean a focus on writing polish and structure (high R1.5 and R1.6), or on conceptual, logical, and evidential aspects (high R1.1 to R1.4), or a comprehensive review across all dimensions?

rationale.R1.1: Conceptual reconstruction is central as reviewing the section involves assessing and potentially reframing the thesis and contribution claims.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is central to ensure the argument chain in the research description holds without contradictions.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central to verify if reported results and evaluations support the stated claims.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central to evaluate the delta against prior works mentioned in the section.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central for reviewing the narrative arc and ordering within the provided section.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central to check sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness in the research text.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship is central to assess reference accuracy and literature coverage in the related work discussion.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is central in reviewing whether limitations or uncertainties are appropriately acknowledged in the results.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is central to identify any exaggerated claims about performance or novelty in the contribution summary.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to ensure the section stays within the bounds of adaptive-inference for object detection.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is peripheral but relevant given potential applications in surveillance like traffic cameras.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is central to review the sourcing of benchmarks and training data mentioned.
